One geographer, [Wilbur Zelinsky](/wiki/Wilbur_Zelinsky "Wilbur Zelinsky"), asserts that the Northeast region lacks a unified cultural identity,[[17]](#cite_note-Zelinksy1-19) but has served as a "culture hearth" for the rest of the nation.[[130]](#cite_note-134) Several much smaller geographical regions within the Northeast have distinct cultural identities.[[17]](#cite_note-Zelinksy1-19)

### Landmarks

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Northeastern_United_States&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Landmarks")]

Almost half of the [National Historic Landmarks](/wiki/National_Historic_Landmark "National Historic Landmark") maintained by the National Park Service are located in the Northeastern United States.[[131]](#cite_note-135)

### Religion

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Northeastern_United_States&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Religion")]

According to a 2009 [Gallup poll](/wiki/Gallup_(company) "Gallup (company)"), the Northeastern states differ from most of the rest of the U.S. in religious affiliation, generally reflecting the descendants of immigration patterns of the late 19th and early 20th centuries, with many Catholics arriving from Ireland, Italy, French Canada - Quebec, Portugal and east-central Europe. Massachusetts, Rhode Island, New York and New Jersey are the only states in the nation where [Catholics](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") outnumber [Protestants](/wiki/Protestant "Protestant") and other [Christian denominations](/wiki/Christian_denomination "Christian denomination"). More than 20% of respondents in Maine, New Hampshire, and Vermont declared no religious identity.[[132]](#cite_note-136) Compared to other U.S. regions, the Northeast, along with the Pacific Northwest, has had the lowest regular religious service attendance and the fewest people for whom religion is an important part of their daily lives as of 2015.[[133]](#cite_note-137)

### Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Northeastern_United_States&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Sports")]

The Northeast region is home to numerous professional sports franchises in the ["Big Four" leagues](/wiki/Major_professional_sports_leagues_in_the_United_States_and_Canada "Major professional sports leagues in the United States and Canada") (NFL, NBA, NHL and MLB),[[134]](#cite_note-138) with more than 100 championships collectively among them.[[135]](#cite_note-139) Professional sports leagues such as the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL), [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") (MLB), [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA), [Women's National Basketball Association](/wiki/Women%27s_National_Basketball_Association "Women's National Basketball Association") (WNBA), [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League") (NHL), [Major League Soccer](/wiki/Major_League_Soccer "Major League Soccer") (MLS), and [National Women's Soccer League](/wiki/National_Women%27s_Soccer_League "National Women's Soccer League") (NWSL), have team franchises in following Northeastern cities:

* New York metropolitan area: [Giants](/wiki/New_York_Giants "New York Giants"), [Jets](/wiki/New_York_Jets "New York Jets") (NFL), [Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees"), [Mets](/wiki/New_York_Mets "New York Mets") (MLB), [Knicks](/wiki/New_York_Knicks "New York Knicks"), [Nets](/wiki/Brooklyn_Nets "Brooklyn Nets") (NBA), [Rangers](/wiki/New_York_Rangers "New York Rangers"), [Islanders](/wiki/New_York_Islanders "New York Islanders"), [Devils](/wiki/New_Jersey_Devils "New Jersey Devils") (NHL), [Red Bulls](/wiki/New_York_Red_Bulls "New York Red Bulls"), [City FC](/wiki/New_York_City_FC "New York City FC") or Pigeons (MLS), [Liberty](/wiki/New_York_Liberty "New York Liberty") (WNBA), [Gotham](/wiki/NJ/NY_Gotham_FC "NJ/NY Gotham FC") or Bats (NWSL)
* Washington: [Commanders](/wiki/Washington_Commanders "Washington Commanders") (NFL), [Nationals](/wiki/Washington_Nationals "Washington Nationals") (MLB), [Wizards](/wiki/Washington_Wizards "Washington Wizards") (NBA), [Capitals](/wiki/Washington_Capitals "Washington Capitals") (NHL), [United](/wiki/D.C._United "D.C. United") (MLS), [Mystics](/wiki/Washington_Mystics "Washington Mystics") (WNBA), [Spirit](/wiki/Washington_Spirit "Washington Spirit") (NWSL)
* Philadelphia: [Eagles](/wiki/Philadelphia_Eagles "Philadelphia Eagles") (NFL), [Phillies](/wiki/Philadelphia_Phillies "Philadelphia Phillies") (MLB), [76ers](/wiki/Philadelphia_76ers "Philadelphia 76ers") (NBA), [Flyers](/wiki/Philadelphia_Flyers "Philadelphia Flyers") (NHL), [Union](/wiki/Philadelphia_Union "Philadelphia Union") (MLS)
* Boston: [Patriots](/wiki/New_England_Patriots "New England Patriots") (NFL), [Red Sox](/wiki/Boston_Red_Sox "Boston Red Sox") (MLB), [Celtics](/wiki/Boston_Celtics "Boston Celtics") (NBA), [Bruins](/wiki/Boston_Bruins "Boston Bruins") (NHL), [Revolution](/wiki/New_England_Revolution "New England Revolution") (MLS)
* Pittsburgh: [Steelers](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Steelers "Pittsburgh Steelers") (NFL), [Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates "Pittsburgh Pirates") (MLB), [Penguins](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Penguins "Pittsburgh Penguins") (NHL)
* Baltimore: [Ravens](/wiki/Baltimore_Ravens "Baltimore Ravens") (NFL), [Orioles](/wiki/Baltimore_Orioles "Baltimore Orioles") (MLB)
* Buffalo: [Bills](/wiki/Buffalo_Bills "Buffalo Bills") (NFL), [Sabres](/wiki/Buffalo_Sabres "Buffalo Sabres") (NHL)
* Uncasville: [Sun](/wiki/Connecticut_Sun "Connecticut Sun") (WNBA)

Notable golf tournaments in the Northeastern United States include [The Northern Trust](/wiki/The_Northern_Trust "The Northern Trust"), [Travelers Championship](/wiki/Travelers_Championship "Travelers Championship"), and [Atlantic City LPGA Classic](/wiki/Atlantic_City_LPGA_Classic "Atlantic City LPGA Classic"). The [US Open](/wiki/US_Open_(tennis) "US Open (tennis)"), held in New York, is one of the four Grand Slam tennis tournaments.

Notable Northeastern motorsports tracks include [Watkins Glen International](/wiki/Watkins_Glen_International "Watkins Glen International"), [Pocono Raceway](/wiki/Pocono_Raceway "Pocono Raceway"), [New Hampshire Motor Speedway](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Motor_Speedway "New Hampshire Motor Speedway") and [Lime Rock Park](/wiki/Lime_Rock_Park "Lime Rock Park"), which have hosted [Formula One](/wiki/Formula_One "Formula One"), [IndyCar](/wiki/IndyCar "IndyCar"), [NASCAR](/wiki/NASCAR "NASCAR") and [International Motor Sports Association](/wiki/International_Motor_Sports_Association "International Motor Sports Association") races. Also, drag strips such as [Englishtown](/wiki/Old_Bridge_Township_Raceway_Park "Old Bridge Township Raceway Park"), [Epping](/wiki/New_England_Dragway "New England Dragway") and [Reading](/wiki/Maple_Grove_Raceway "Maple Grove Raceway") have hosted [NHRA](/wiki/NHRA "NHRA") national events. [Belmont Park](/wiki/Belmont_Park "Belmont Park") at New York hosts the [Belmont Stakes](/wiki/Belmont_Stakes "Belmont Stakes") horse races, which is part of the [Triple Crown of Thoroughbred Racing](/wiki/Triple_Crown_of_Thoroughbred_Racing_(United_States) "Triple Crown of Thoroughbred Racing (United States)").

The region has also been noted for the prevalence of the traditionally Northeastern sports of [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") and [lacrosse](/wiki/Lacrosse "Lacrosse").[[136]](#cite_note-140)

## Politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Northeastern_United_States&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Politics")]

Main article: [Political party strength in U.S. states](/wiki/Political_party_strength_in_U.S._states "Political party strength in U.S. states")

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Northeastern_United_States&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-NAVD88_5-0)** Elevation adjusted to [North American Vertical Datum of 1988](/wiki/North_American_Vertical_Datum_of_1988 "North American Vertical Datum of 1988").
2. **[^](#cite_ref-11)** The term "East Coast" is often used to refer to the [Northeastern megalopolis](/wiki/Northeast_megalopolis "Northeast megalopolis"). "East Coast" primarily refers to the region between [Washington, DC](/wiki/Washington,_DC "Washington, DC"), in the south and [Boston](/wiki/Boston "Boston") in the north.[[7]](#cite_note-8)[[8]](#cite_note-9)[[9]](#cite_note-10)
3. **[^](#cite_ref-62)** The U.S. Census Bureau reorganized its administrative units, and its regional offices do not cover the Census regions (the northeasternmost regional office headquartered in New York covers New England, New York, New Jersey, and Puerto Rico).[[59]](#cite_note-61)
4. **[^](#cite_ref-96)** Based on U.S. Census Bureau population projections to 2030 (and assuming constant land area) the population density for the [South Atlantic](/wiki/South_Atlantic_states "South Atlantic states") division will increase significantly to 294.6/mi2, New England's density will increase to 249.2/mi2 and the East North Central division will increase only slightly to 200.2/mi2. The division with the 5th highest density is projected to be the [East South Central](/wiki/East_South_Central_States "East South Central States") division at 111.6/mi2.[[92]](#cite_note-census.gov-95)
